growing_areas: All Agro-Climatic Zones Of Sri Lanka Except Upcountry Wet Zone

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Transmits Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV), causing upward curling of leaves, yellow leaf margins, smaller leaves, stunted plants, and flower drop. No fruit set if infected at early stages.
pests.solution: Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

pests.name: Leafhopper
pests.impact: Transmits Curlitop Virus (CTV), causing leaves to curl upwards along the midrib. Petioles and midribs gradually curve downwards, showing leaf inclination but no wilting. Leaves become thick, crinkled, brittle, and dark green with purple veins.
pests.solution: Use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

pests.name: Thrips
pests.impact: Transmits Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus (TSWV), causing leaves to turn bronze and wrinkle, with necrotic streaks and spots. Dark brown streaks appear on leaf veins, stems, and growing tips. Plants are small and stunted. Ripe fruits show light red or yellow areas. Severe necrotic conditions can lead to plant death.
pests.solution: Remove weeds, remove old crop debris, use healthy seeds, crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.impact: Transmits Cucumber Mosaic Virus (CMV), causing early yellowing and severe stunting, shoestring-like leaf blades, small fruits, reduced and delayed ripening, and leaf spots.
pests.solution: Reduce vector population using insecticides, maintain weed-free fields and field hygiene, use disease-free seeds.

diseases.name: Damping Off
diseases.causal_agent: Phythium Spp., Phytophthora Spp., Fusarium Spp., Rhizoctonia Spp., Sclerotium Spp., Colletotrichum Spp.
diseases.impact: Seedling death due to root infection.
diseases.solution: Set nursery beds in land not previously cultivated with Solanaceae crops. Heat sterilize nursery beds. Seed treatment with Thiram 80WP 5g/1Kg or Homai 4g/1kg. Soil treatment with Thiram 80WP 7g/5 l/m-2 or Homai 5g/5 l/m-2.

diseases.name: Early Blight
diseases.impact: Spiral spots on leaves, fruits, and stems.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages: Mancozeb 80% WP at 20g/10l, Metalaxyl 8%+ Mancozeb 64% WP at 12.5g/10l, Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WG at 8ml/10l.

diseases.name: Late Blight
diseases.impact: Rapid death of leaves, stems, and fruits. Gray wet spots on lower mature leaves, turning black with fungal growth.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages: Mancozeb 80% WP at 20g/10l, Metalaxyl 8%+ Mancozeb 64% WP at 12.5g/10l, Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WG at 8ml/10l.

diseases.name: Target Spot
diseases.impact: Round, dark brown necrotic spots with a yellow halo on leaves, spreading to all leaflets. Leaves turn yellow, drop rapidly, and die. Spots also appear on stems and fruits.
diseases.solution: Suitable fungicides are currently under research.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Light green to bright yellow spots on the upper leaf surface. White powdery spots develop on the upper surface when fungal spores form. Severe infection leads to leaf drop.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages: 80% WG Sulfur at 50 g/10 l water, Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC at 30 ml/10 l water, Carbendazim 50% WP at 7 g/10 l water.

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.impact: Plant death from apical buds, flower drop. Black, sunken spots on leaves, stems, and fruits. Fruit rot.
diseases.solution: Perform seed treatment with recommended fungicides before sowing. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
diseases.impact: Wet spots on affected leaves become round, with centers turning from brown to gray.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides: Daconil 15-30 ml/10L, Mancozeb 20 ml/10L, Topsin 6g/10L, Carbendazim 7g/10L.

diseases.name: Collar Rot / Root Rot
diseases.causal_agent: Fusarium Spp., Rhizoctonia Spp., Sclerotium Spp.
diseases.impact: The pathogen damages the base of the plant, leading to drying of lower leaves, and eventually the entire plant dries and shows general wilting symptoms, which ultimately leads to death.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Plant shows permanent wilting even with optimal soil moisture. A viscous exudate is released from the cut stem when immersed in water.
diseases.solution: Use resistant varieties, practice crop rotation with legumes or cereals, maintain field hygiene.

diseases.name: Bacterial Canker
diseases.impact: Tomato leaf margins turn brown with a yellow border. Lower leaves often wilt on only one side. The entire plant may collapse and die. Brown streaks are visible in the vascular system when the stem is cut open. The stem cracks, forming brown scars. Internal stem discoloration occurs. Cream or white spots with small brown centers (bird's eye spots) appear on fruits.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, practice crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

diseases.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (Tylcv)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves. Chlorotic (yellow) leaf margins. Leaves are smaller than normal. Tomato plants are stunted, and flowers drop. If infected at early stages, no fruit set occurs. Infected plants may appear randomly throughout the field.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, practice crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

diseases.name: Curlitop Virus (Ctv)
diseases.impact: Leaves curl upwards along the midrib. Petioles and midribs gradually curve downwards, showing leaf inclination but no wilting. Leaves become thick, crinkled, brittle, and dark green with purple veins.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, practice crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

diseases.name: Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus (Tswv)
diseases.impact: Leaves turn bronze and wrinkle, with necrotic streaks and spots. Dark brown streaks appear on leaf veins, stems, and growing tips. Plants are small and stunted. Ripe fruits show light red or yellow areas. In severe necrotic conditions, plants die.
diseases.solution: Remove weeds, remove old crop debris, use healthy seeds, practice crop rotation, maintain field hygiene.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (Cmv)
diseases.impact: Early stages show yellowing and severe stunting, leading to a bushy appearance. Later, shoestring-like leaf blades develop. Plants are stunted, leaves yellow and thin, fruits are small, and ripening is reduced and delayed. Leaf spots may also occur.
diseases.solution: Use disease-free seeds, maintain weed-free fields and field hygiene, reduce vector population using insecticides.
